title: Rank Preserving Structural Failure Time Models
text: clinical trials, treatment switching occurs patients control group switch experimental treatment experiencing disease progression clinical criteria. treatment switching can provide ethical benefits trial participants, introduces significant challenges estimating true effect experimental treatment overall survival (OS). left unadjusted, treatment switching can obscure real difference treatment arms lead biased estimates, typically underestimating treatment benefit. rank preserving structural failure time model (RPSFTM) statistical approach developed address issue. RPSFTM adjusts effects treatment switching modeling survival times patients switched treatments remained control treatment. model assumes treatment effect regardless patient received treatment. rank-preserving property means relative ordering counterfactural survival times experimental treatment relative ordering counterfactural survival times control treatment. Formally, let \(Y_i^{=1}\) represent potential outcome subject \(\) treatment condition \(=1\) (experimental), \(Y_i^{=0}\) represent potential outcome treatment condition \(=0\) (control). ranking \(\{Y_i^{=1}: =1,\ldots,n\}\) identical ranking \(\{Y_i^{=0}: =1,\ldots,n\}\), say rank preservation holds. structural failure time model refers framework used estimate true (unobserved) survival time absence treatment switching, assuming experimental treatment multiplicative effect survival. Specifically, patient’s observed survival time, \(T_i\), divided time spent control treatment, \(T_{C_i}\), time spent experimental treatment, \(T_{E_i}\), \(T_i = T_{C_i} + T_{E_i}\). rx parameter rpsftm function represents proportion time spent experimental treatment, defined ratio \(T_{E_i}/T_i\). structural model counterfactual untreated survival times expressed \[U_{,\psi} = T_{C_i} + e^{\psi} T_{E_i},\] three distinct cases one-way treatment switching control arm experimental arm: Experimental Group Patients: \(T_{C_i} = 0\), \(T_{E_i}\) represents time randomization either death censoring. Control Group Nonswitchers: \(T_{C_i}\) time randomization either death censoring, \(T_{E_i} = 0\). Control Group Switchers: \(T_{C_i}\) time randomization treatment switch, \(T_{E_i}\) time switch either death censoring.

title: Rank Preserving Structural Failure Time Models
text: censoring time \(C_i\) must defined patients including experience event. assume censoring non-informative absence treatment switching, .e., \(T_{L_i} \perp\!\!\!\perp C_i\), \(T_{L_i}\) denotes latent time event subject \(\). observed time event censoring given \[T_i = \min(T_{C_i} + e^{-\psi}(T_{L_i} - T_{C_i}), C_i)\] event indicator \[\Delta_{} = (T_{C_i} + e^{-\psi}(T_{L_i} - T_{C_i})\leq C_i)\] patient switches treatment, counterfactual time event censoring \[U_{,\psi} = \min(T_{L_i}, T_{C_i} + e^{\psi}(C_i - T_{C_i}))\] event indicator can rewritten \[\Delta_{} = (T_{L_i} \leq  T_{C_i} + e^{\psi}(C_i - T_{C_i}))\] However, treatment switching often associated poor prognosis survival, .e., \(T_{L_i}\) \(T_{C_i}\) correlated. Consequently, \(T_{L_i}\) \(T_{C_i} + e^{\psi}(C_i - T_{C_i})\) also correlated. result, using sample \(\{(U_{,\psi},\Delta_i)\}\) generally produce biased estimate survival distribution \(T_{L_i}\). address issue, define recensoring time accounts possible switching times, \[   D_{,\psi}^* = \min_{T_{C_i} \[0, C_i]} \{T_{C_i} + e^{\psi}(C_i - T_{C_i})\} = \min(C_i, e^{\psi}C_i) \] recensored time event censoring \[U_{,\psi}^* = \min(U_{,\psi}, D_{,\psi}^*) = \min(T_{L_i}, D_{,\psi}^*)\] corresponding event indicator \[\Delta_{,\psi}^* = (U_{,\psi} \leq D_{,\psi}^*) = (T_{L_i} \leq D_{,\psi}^*)\] construction, \(T_{L_i} \perp\!\!\!\perp D_{,\psi}^*\), thus sample \(\{(U_{,\psi}^*,\Delta_{,\psi}^*)\}\) provides unbiased estimate survival distribution \(T_{L_i}\). important note recensoring applied switchers, sample becomes \[U_{,\psi}^{\dagger} = \min(T_{L_i}, D_{,\psi}^* (S_i=1) + C_i (S_i=0))\] \[\Delta_{,\psi}^{\dagger} = (T_{L_i} \leq D_{,\psi}^* (S_i=1) + C_i (S_i=0))\] \(S_i\) indicator treatment switching. text: data input data frame contains following variables: id: id identify observations belonging subject counting process data time-dependent covariates. stratum: stratum. tstart: starting time time interval counting-process data time-dependent covariates. tstop: stopping time time interval counting-process data time-dependent covariates. event: event indicator, 1=event, 0=event. treat: randomized treatment indicator, 1=treatment, 0=control. swtrt: treatment switch indicator, 1=switch, 0=switch. swtrt_time: time randomization treatment switch. base_cov: baseline covariates (excluding treat) used outcome model. numerator: baseline covariates (excluding treat) used numerator switching model stabilized weights. denominator: baseline (excluding treat) time-dependent covariates used denominator switching model. id name id variable input data. stratum name(s) stratum variable(s) input data. tstart name tstart variable input data. tstop name tstop variable input data. event name event variable input data. treat name treatment variable input data. swtrt name swtrt variable input data. swtrt_time name swtrt_time variable input data. base_cov names baseline covariates (excluding treat) input data Cox model. numerator names baseline covariates (excluding treat) input data numerator switching model stabilized weights. denominator names baseline  (excluding treat) time-dependent covariates input data denominator switching model. logistic_switching_model Whether pooled logistic regression switching model used. strata_main_effect_only Whether include strata main effects logistic regression switching model. Defaults TRUE, otherwise possible strata combinations considered switching model. ns_df Degrees freedom natural cubic spline visit-specific intercepts pooled logistic regression model. Defaults 3 two internal knots 33 67 percentiles treatment switching times. firth Whether Firth's bias reducing penalized likelihood used. flic Whether apply intercept correction obtain accurate predicted probabilities. stabilized_weights Whether use stabilized weights. default TRUE. trunc truncation fraction weight distribution. title: Inverse Probability of Censoring Weights (IPCW) for Treatment Switching — ipcw
text: hazard ratio confidence interval -switching scenario obtained follows: Exclude observations treatment switch. Define crossover event indicators last time interval subject. time-dependent Cox switching models, replicate unique event times across treatment arms within subject. Fit denominator switching model (numerator model stabilized weights) estimate inverse probability censoring weights. Either Cox model time-dependent covariates pooled logistic regression model can used. pooled logistic regression model, probability remaining uncensored (.e., switching) calculated \(1 - \hat{p}_{\text{switch}}\) accumulated time start interval. time-dependent Cox model, probability remaining unswitched derived estimated baseline hazard predicted risk score end interval. Fit weighted Cox model outcome survival times (excluding data switching) estimate hazard ratio. Construct p-value confidence interval hazard ratio using either robust sandwich variance bootstrapping. bootstrapping used, confidence interval p-value based t-distribution n_boot - 1 degrees freedom.

text: data input data frame contains following variables: id: subject id. stratum: stratum. time: survival time right censored data. event: event indicator, 1=event, 0=event. treat: randomized treatment indicator, 1=treatment, 0=control. rx: proportion time active treatment. censor_time: administrative censoring time. provided subjects including events. base_cov: baseline covariates (excluding treat). id name id variable input data. stratum name(s) stratum variable(s) input data. time name time variable input data. event name event variable input data. treat name treatment variable input data. rx name rx variable input data. censor_time name censor_time variable input data. base_cov names baseline covariates (excluding treat) input data causal AFT model outcome Cox model. aft_dist assumed distribution time event AFT model. Options include "exponential", "weibull" (default), "loglogistic", "lognormal". strata_main_effect_only Whether include strata main effects AFT model. Defaults TRUE, otherwise possible strata combinations considered AFT model. low_psi lower limit causal parameter. hi_psi upper limit causal parameter. treat_modifier optional sensitivity parameter constant treatment effect assumption. recensor Whether apply recensoring counterfactual survival times. Defaults TRUE. admin_recensor_only Whether apply recensoring administrative censoring times . Defaults TRUE. FALSE, recensoring applied actual censoring times dropouts. autoswitch Whether exclude recensoring treatment arms switching. Defaults TRUE. root_finding Character string specifying univariate root-finding algorithm use. Options "brent" (default) Brent's method, "bisection" bisection method. alpha significance level calculate confidence intervals. ties method handling ties Cox model, either "breslow" "efron" (default). tol desired accuracy (convergence tolerance) psi root finding algorithm. boot Whether use bootstrap obtain confidence interval hazard ratio. Defaults FALSE, case, confidence interval constructed match log-rank test p-value. n_boot number bootstrap samples. seed seed reproduce bootstrap results. nthreads number threads use bootstrapping (0 means default RcppParallel behavior).

Sstar: data frame containing counterfactual untreated survival times event indicators treatment group. variables include id, stratum, "t_star", "d_star", "treated", base_cov, treat. kmstar: data frame containing Kaplan-Meier estimates based counterfactual untreated survival times treatment arm. data_aft: input data AFT model estimating psi. variables include id, stratum, "t_star", "d_star", "treated", base_cov, treat. fit_aft: fitted AFT model estimating psi. res_aft: deviance residuals fitted AFT model. data_outcome: input data outcome Cox model counterfactual unswitched survival times. variables include id, stratum, "t_star", "d_star", "treated", base_cov, treat. km_outcome: Kaplan-Meier estimates survival functions treatment control groups based counterfactual unswitched survival times. lr_outcome: log-rank test results treatment effect based counterfactual unswitched survival times. fit_outcome: fitted outcome Cox model. fail: Whether model fails converge. psimissing: Whether psi parameter estimated. settings: list containing input parameter values. fail_boots: indicators failed bootstrap samples boot TRUE. fail_boots_data: data failed bootstrap samples boot TRUE. hr_boots: bootstrap hazard ratio estimates boot TRUE. psi_boots: bootstrap psi estimates boot TRUE.

title: Iterative Parameter Estimation (IPE) for Treatment Switching — ipe
text: Assuming one-way switching control treatment, hazard ratio confidence interval -switching scenario obtained follows: Estimate causal parameter \(\psi\) iteratively fitting AFT model observed survival times treatment arm counterfactual survival times control arm: $$U_{,\psi} = T_{C_i} + e^{\psi}T_{E_i}$$ Compute counterfactual survival times control patients using estimated \(\psi\). Fit Cox model observed survival times treatment group counterfactual survival times control group estimate hazard ratio. Obtain confidence interval hazard ratio using either ITT log-rank test p-value bootstrap. bootstrapping, interval p-value derived t-distribution n_boot - 1 degrees freedom.

title: Iterative Parameter Estimation (IPE) for Treatment Switching — ipe
text: Michael Branson John Whitehead. Estimating treatment effect survival studies patients switch treatment. Statistics Medicine. 2002;21(17):2449-2463. Ian R White. Letter Editor: Estimating treatment effects randomized trials treatment switching. Statistics Medicine. 2006;25(9):1619-1622.

title: Logistic Regression Models for Binary Data — logisregr
text: Fitting logistic regression model using Firth's bias reduction method equivalent penalization log-likelihood Jeffreys prior. Firth's penalized log-likelihood given $$l(\beta) + \frac{1}{2} \log(\mbox{det}((\beta)))$$ components gradient \(g(\beta)\) computed $$g(\beta_j) + \frac{1}{2} \mbox{trace}\left((\beta)^{-1} \frac{\partial (\beta)}{\partial \beta_j}\right)$$ Hessian matrix modified penalty. Firth's method reduces bias maximum likelihood estimates coefficients, introduces bias toward one-half predicted probabilities. straightforward modification Firth’s logistic regression achieve unbiased average predicted probabilities involves post hoc adjustment intercept. approach, known Firth’s logistic regression intercept correction (FLIC), preserves bias-corrected effect estimates. excluding intercept penalization, ensures sacrifice accuracy effect estimates improve predictions.

text: list following components: sumstat: data frame summary statistics model fit following variables: n: number observations. nevents: number events. loglik0: (penalized) log-likelihood null. loglik1: maximum (penalized) log-likelihood. scoretest: score test statistic. niter: number Newton-Raphson iterations. ties: method handling ties, either "breslow" "efron". p: number columns Cox model design matrix. robust: Whether use robust variance estimate. firth: Whether use Firth's penalized likelihood method. fail: Whether model fails converge. loglik0_unpenalized: unpenalized log-likelihood null. loglik1_unpenalized: maximum unpenalized log-likelihood. parest: data frame parameter estimates following variables: param: name covariate parameter estimate. beta: log hazard ratio estimate. sebeta: standard error log hazard ratio estimate. z: Wald test statistic log hazard ratio. expbeta: hazard ratio estimate. lower: lower limit confidence interval. upper: upper limit confidence interval. p: p-value chi-square test. method: method compute confidence interval p-value. sebeta_naive: naive standard error log hazard ratio estimate robust variance requested. basehaz: data frame baseline hazards following variables (est_basehaz TRUE): time: observed event time. nrisk: number patients risk time point. nevent: number events time point. haz: baseline hazard time point. varhaz: variance baseline hazard time point assuming parameter beta known. gradhaz: gradient baseline hazard respect beta time point (presence covariates). stratum: stratum. residuals: martingale residuals. linear_predictors: vector linear predictors. p: number parameters. param: parameter names. beta: parameter estimate. vbeta: covariance matrix parameter estimates. vbeta_naive: naive covariance matrix parameter estimates. terms: terms object. xlevels: record levels factors used fitting. settings: list containing input parameter values.

title: Prepare Survival Data With Time-Dependent Covariates — preptdc
text: function prepares counting-process style survival dataset analyses time-dependent covariates. merges baseline longitudinal data, fills missing covariate values using last-observation-carried-forward (LOCF), restricts time points covariates change (optional), constructs tstart, tstop, event variables suitable use survival models.

title: Prepare Survival Data With Time-Dependent Covariates — preptdc
code: preptdc(   adsl,   adtdc,   id = "SUBJID",   randdt = "RANDDT",   trtsdt = "TRTSDT",   pddt = "PDDT",   xodt = "XODT",   osdt = "OSDT",   died = "DIED",   dcutdt = "DCUTDT",   paramcd = "PARAMCD",   adt = "ADT",   aval = "AVAL",   nodup = TRUE,   offset = TRUE,   nthreads = 0 )

title: Prepare Survival Data With Time-Dependent Covariates — preptdc
text: adsl data set containing baseline subject-level information. include, minimum, subject ID (id), randomization date (randdt), treatment start date (trtsdt), progression date (pddt), treatment switch date (xodt), survival outcome (osdt, died), data cut-date (dcutdt). adtdc data set containing longitudinal time-dependent covariate data, subject ID (id), parameter code (paramcd), analysis date (adt), covariate value (aval). id Character string specifying column name subject ID. randdt Character string specifying column name randomization date. trtsdt Character string specifying column name treatment start date. pddt Character string specifying column name progression date. xodt Character string specifying column name treatment crossover/switch date. osdt Character string specifying column name overall survival date (death date last known alive date). died Character string specifying column name death indicator (0 = alive/censored, 1 = died). dcutdt Character string specifying column name data cut-date. paramcd Character string specifying column name parameter code (identifying different covariates). adt Character string specifying column name analysis date time-dependent covariate dataset. aval Character string specifying column name analysis value (covariate values). nodup Logical; TRUE (default), rows least one covariate changes compared previous row (within subject) retained, along first row per subject (baseline). offset Logical; TRUE (default), add 1-day offset computing analysis day variables (ady, osdy, etc.). nthreads Integer number threads use `data.table' (0 means default data.table behavior).

title: Prepare Survival Data With Time-Dependent Covariates — preptdc
text: data set one row per subject time interval, including: tstart, tstop — interval start stop times (days randomization). event — event indicator (0/1). Covariates expanded wide format. Auxiliary variables progression indicator (pd), treatment switch indicator (swtrt), administrative censoring time.

title: Prepare Survival Data With Time-Dependent Covariates — preptdc
text: function performs following steps: Merge adsl adtdc obtain randomization date treatment start date. Define adt2 adt adt > trtsdt, randdt adt <= trtsdt (.e., baseline time point). ensures baseline covariate value last non-missing value treatment start date. Post-baseline covariate values anchored actual analysis dates. first record per subject corresponds survival time zero randomization ensures availability baseline covariates randomization. Keep last record per subject, adt2, paramcd. Construct complete skeleton covariates present subject time point. Fill missing covariate values using LOCF. Pivot wide format one row per subject time point. Optionally drop rows without covariate changes (nodup = TRUE). Merge survival outcomes adsl. Compute time--event variables (ady, osdy, etc.), well counting-process style variables tstart, tstop, event.

text: nsim Number simulated datasets. n Number subjects per simulation. shape Shape parameter Weibull distribution time death. scale Scale parameter Weibull distribution time death control group. gamma Rate parameter exponential distribution random dropouts control group. tfmin Minimum planned follow-time (days). tfmax Maximum planned follow-time (days). psi Log time ratio death time control vs experimental treatment. omega Log time ratio dropout time control vs experimental treatment. pswitch Probability treatment switching disease progression. Shape parameter 1 Beta distribution time disease progression fraction time death. b Shape parameter 2 Beta distribution time disease progression. low_psi Lower bound search interval causal parameter \(\psi\). hi_psi Upper bound search interval causal parameter \(\psi\). treat_modifier Sensitivity parameter modifying constant treatment effect assumption. recensor_type Type recensoring apply: 0: recensoring 1: Recensor control-arm subjects 2: Recensor switchers control arm 3: Recensor control-arm switchers whose counterfactual survival exceeds planned follow-time admin_recensor_only Logical. TRUE, recensoring applied administrative censoring times. FALSE, also applied dropout times. autoswitch Logical. TRUE, disables recensoring arms without treatment switching. alpha Significance level confidence interval calculation (default 0.05). ties Method handling tied event times Cox model. Options "efron" (default) "breslow". tol Convergence tolerance root-finding estimation \(\psi\). boot Logical. TRUE, bootstrap used estimate confidence interval hazard ratio. FALSE, confidence interval matched log-rank p-value. n_boot Number bootstrap samples, used boot = TRUE. seed Optional. Random seed reproducibility.

title: Rank Preserving Structural Failure Time Model (RPSFTM) for Treatment Switching — rpsftm
text: data input data frame contains following variables: id: subject id. stratum: stratum. time: survival time right censored data. event: event indicator, 1=event, 0=event. treat: randomized treatment indicator, 1=treatment, 0=control. rx: proportion time active treatment. censor_time: administrative censoring time. provided subjects including events. base_cov: baseline covariates (excluding treat). id name id variable input data. stratum name(s) stratum variable(s) input data. time name time variable input data. event name event variable input data. treat name treatment variable input data. rx name rx variable input data. censor_time name censor_time variable input data. base_cov names baseline covariates (excluding treat) input data outcome Cox model. covariates also used Cox model estimating psi psi_test = "phreg" parametric survival regression/AFT model estimating psi psi_test = "lifereg". psi_test survival function calculate Z-statistic, e.g., "logrank" (default), "phreg", "lifereg". aft_dist assumed distribution time event AFT model psi_test = "lifereg". Options include "exponential", "weibull" (default), "loglogistic", "lognormal". strata_main_effect_only Whether include strata main effects AFT model. Defaults TRUE, otherwise possible strata combinations considered AFT model. low_psi lower limit causal parameter. hi_psi upper limit causal parameter. n_eval_z number points low_psi hi_psi (inclusive) evaluate Z-statistics. treat_modifier optional sensitivity parameter constant treatment effect assumption. recensor Whether apply recensoring counterfactual survival times. Defaults TRUE. admin_recensor_only Whether apply recensoring administrative censoring times . Defaults TRUE. FALSE, recensoring applied actual censoring times dropouts. autoswitch Whether exclude recensoring treatment arms switching. Defaults TRUE. gridsearch Whether use grid search estimate causal parameter psi. Defaults TRUE, otherwise, root finding algorithm used. root_finding Character string specifying univariate root-finding algorithm use. Options "brent" (default) Brent's method, "bisection" bisection method. alpha significance level calculate confidence intervals. ties method handling ties Cox model, either "breslow" "efron" (default). tol desired accuracy (convergence tolerance) psi root finding algorithm. boot Whether use bootstrap obtain confidence interval hazard ratio. Defaults FALSE, case, confidence interval constructed match log-rank test p-value. n_boot number bootstrap samples. seed seed reproduce bootstrap results. nthreads number threads use bootstrapping (0 means default RcppParallel behavior).

title: Rank Preserving Structural Failure Time Model (RPSFTM) for Treatment Switching — rpsftm
text: list following components: psi: estimated causal parameter. psi_roots: Vector psi values Z-statistic zero, identified using grid search linear interpolation. psi_CI: confidence interval psi. psi_CI_type: type confidence interval psi, .e., "grid search", "root finding", "bootstrap". pvalue: two-sided p-value. pvalue_type: type two-sided p-value treatment effect, .e., "log-rank" "bootstrap". hr: estimated hazard ratio Cox model. hr_CI: confidence interval hazard ratio. hr_CI_type: type confidence interval hazard ratio, either "log-rank p-value" "bootstrap". event_summary: data frame containing count percentage deaths switches treatment arm. eval_z: data frame containing Z-statistics treatment effect evaluated sequence psi values. Used plot check range psi values search solution limits confidence interval psi need modified. Sstar: data frame containing counterfactual untreated survival times event indicators treatment group. variables include id, stratum, "t_star", "d_star", "treated", base_cov, treat. kmstar: data frame containing Kaplan-Meier estimates based counterfactual untreated survival times treatment arm. data_outcome: input data outcome Cox model counterfactual unswitched survival times. variables include id, stratum, "t_star", "d_star", "treated", base_cov, treat. km_outcome: Kaplan-Meier estimates survival functions treatment control groups based counterfactual unswitched survival times. lr_outcome: log-rank test results treatment effect based counterfactual unswitched survival times. fit_outcome: fitted outcome Cox model. fail: Whether model fails converge. psimissing: Whether psi parameter estimated. settings: list containing input parameter values. fail_boots: indicators failed bootstrap samples boot TRUE. fail_boots_data: data failed bootstrap samples boot TRUE. hr_boots: bootstrap hazard ratio estimates boot TRUE. psi_boots: bootstrap psi estimates boot TRUE.

title: Rank Preserving Structural Failure Time Model (RPSFTM) for Treatment Switching — rpsftm
text: Assuming one-way switching control treatment, hazard ratio confidence interval -switching scenario obtained follows: Estimate causal parameter \(\psi\) using g-estimation based log-rank test (default), Cox model, parametric survival/AFT model, using counterfactual untreated survival times arms: $$U_{,\psi} = T_{C_i} + e^{\psi}T_{E_i}$$ Compute counterfactual survival times control patients using estimated \(\psi\). Fit Cox model observed survival times treatment group counterfactual survival times control group estimate hazard ratio. Obtain confidence interval hazard ratio using either ITT log-rank test p-value bootstrap. bootstrapping, interval p-value derived t-distribution n_boot - 1 degrees freedom. grid search used estimate \(\psi\), estimated \(\psi\) one smallest absolute value among Z-statistic zero based linear interpolation. root finding used, estimated \(\psi\) solution equation Z-statistic zero.

code: library(dplyr)  # Example 1: one-way treatment switching (control to active)  data <- immdef %>% mutate(rx = 1-xoyrs/progyrs)  fit1 <- rpsftm(   data, id = "id", time = "progyrs", event = "prog", treat = "imm",   rx = "rx", censor_time = "censyrs", boot = FALSE)  fit1 #>             n event_n event_pct switch_n switch_pct event_out_n event_out_pct #> Control   500     169      33.8      189       37.8         142          28.4 #> Treatment 500     143      28.6        0        0.0         143          28.6 #>           imm #> Control     0 #> Treatment   1 #>  #>                            Estimate Lower 95% Upper 95% #> Causal parameter psi       -0.187   -0.347    0.004     #> Causal survival time ratio 1.206    0.996     1.415     #> Hazard ratio (HR)          0.759    0.572     1.007     #> P-value (log-rank)         0.0556                        # Example 2: two-way treatment switching (illustration only)  # the eventual survival time shilong1 <- shilong %>%   arrange(bras.f, id, tstop) %>%   group_by(bras.f, id) %>%   slice(n()) %>%   select(-c("ps", "ttc", "tran"))  shilong2 <- shilong1 %>%   mutate(rx = ifelse(co, ifelse(bras.f == "MTA", dco/ady,                                  1 - dco/ady),                      ifelse(bras.f == "MTA", 1, 0)))  fit2 <- rpsftm(   shilong2, id = "id", time = "tstop", event = "event",   treat = "bras.f", rx = "rx", censor_time = "dcut",   base_cov = c("agerand", "sex.f", "tt_Lnum", "rmh_alea.c",                "pathway.f"),   low_psi = -3, hi_psi = 3, boot = FALSE)  fit2 #>             n event_n event_pct switch_n switch_pct event_out_n event_out_pct #> Control    93      63      67.7       68       73.1          58          62.4 #> Treatment 100      67      67.0       25       25.0          62          62.0 #>           bras.f #> Control       CT #> Treatment    MTA #>  #>                            Estimate Lower 95% Upper 95% #> Causal parameter psi       1.013    -0.338    2.095     #> Causal survival time ratio 0.363    0.123     1.401     #> Hazard ratio (HR)          2.820    0.609     13.062    #> P-value (log-rank)         0.1851

code: library(dplyr)  # modify pd and dpd based on co and dco shilong <- shilong %>%   mutate(dpd = ifelse(co & !pd, dco, dpd),          pd = ifelse(co & !pd, 1, pd)) %>%   mutate(dpd = ifelse(pd & co & dco < dpd, dco, dpd))    # the eventual survival time shilong1 <- shilong %>%   arrange(bras.f, id, tstop) %>%   group_by(bras.f, id) %>%   slice(n()) %>%   select(-c("ps", "ttc", "tran"))  # the last value of time-dependent covariates before pd shilong2 <- shilong %>%   filter(pd == 0 | tstart <= dpd) %>%   arrange(bras.f, id, tstop) %>%   group_by(bras.f, id) %>%   slice(n()) %>%   select(bras.f, id, ps, ttc, tran)  # combine baseline and time-dependent covariates shilong3 <- shilong1 %>%   left_join(shilong2, by = c("bras.f", "id"))  # apply the two-stage method fit1 <- tsesimp(   data = shilong3, id = "id", time = "tstop", event = "event",   treat = "bras.f", censor_time = "dcut", pd = "pd",   pd_time = "dpd", swtrt = "co", swtrt_time = "dco",   base_cov = c("agerand", "sex.f", "tt_Lnum", "rmh_alea.c",                 "pathway.f"),   base2_cov = c("agerand", "sex.f", "tt_Lnum", "rmh_alea.c",                 "pathway.f", "ps", "ttc", "tran"),   aft_dist = "weibull", alpha = 0.05,   recensor = TRUE, swtrt_control_only = FALSE, offset = 1,   boot = FALSE)  fit1 #>             n event_n event_pct pd_n pd_pct switch_n switch_pct event_out_n #> Control    93      63      67.7   85   91.4       68       73.1          58 #> Treatment 100      67      67.0   83   83.0       25       25.0          63 #>           event_out_pct bras.f #> Control            62.4     CT #> Treatment          63.0    MTA #>  #>                                              Estimate Lower 95% Upper 95% #> Causal parameter psi for control arm         -1.068   -1.534    -0.601    #> Causal survival time ratio for control arm   2.909    1.824     4.637     #> Causal parameter psi for treatment arm       -0.984   -1.516    -0.451    #> Causal survival time ratio for treatment arm 2.674    1.570     4.555     #> Hazard ratio (HR)                            0.913    0.636     1.310     #> P-value (Cox model)                          0.6200

text: simulation algorithm adapted Xu et al. (2022) simulates disease progression status incorporating multiplicative effects baseline time-dependent covariates survival time. design options tdxo coxo specify timing treatment switching study arm eligibility switching, respectively. Time measured days. fixed follow-design, subjects share follow-duration. contrast, variable follow-design, follow-time also depends subject's enrollment date. number treatment cycles subject determined dividing follow-time number days cycle. randomization, subjects assigned treatment arms using block randomization, allocation1 patients assigned active treatment allocation2 control within randomized block. baseline covariate also generated subject: $$X_i \sim \mbox{Bernoulli}(p_1 R_i + p_0 (1-R_i))$$ initial survival time drawn exponential distribution hazard: $$\lambda_T \exp(\beta_1 R_i + \beta_2 X_i)$$ define event indicator cycle \(j\) $$Y_{,j} = (T_i \leq j\times days)$$ initial states set \(L_{,0} = 0\), \(Z_{,0} = 0\), \(A_{,0} = 0\), \(Y_{,0} = 0\). treatment cycle \(j=1,\ldots,J\), set \(tstart = (j-1) \times days\). Generate time-dependent covariates: $$\mbox{logit} P(L_{,j}=1|\mbox{history}) =   \gamma_0 + \gamma_1 A_{,j-1} + \gamma_2 L_{,j-1} +   \gamma_3 X_i + \gamma_4 R_i$$ \(T_i \leq j \times days\), set \(tstop = T_i\) \(Y_{,j} = 1\), completes data generation subject \(\). \(T_i > j \times days\), set \(tstop = j\times days\), \(Y_{,j} = 0\), perform following proceeding next cycle subject. Generate disease progression status: \(Z_{,j-1} = 0\), $$\mbox{logit} P(Z_{,j}=1 | \mbox{history}) = \zeta_0 +   \zeta_1 L_{,j} + \zeta_2 X_i + \zeta_3 R_i$$ Otherwise, set \(Z_{,j} = 1\). Generate alternative therapy status: \(A_{,j-1} = 0\), determine switching eligibility based design options. switching allowed: $$\mbox{logit} P(A_{,j} = 1 | \mbox{history}) = \alpha_0 +   \alpha_1 L_{,j} + \alpha_2 X_i$$ switching now allowed, set \(A_{,j} = 0\). \(A_{,j-1} = 1\), set \(A_{,j} = 1\) (switched alternative therapy, remain alternative therapy). Update survival time based changes alternative therapy status time-dependent covariates: $$T_i = j\times days + (T_i - j\times days) \exp\{   -(\theta_{1,1}R_i + \theta_{1,0}(1-R_i))(A_{,j} - A_{,j-1})   -\theta_2 (L_{,j} - L_{,j-1})\}$$ Additional random censoring times generated exponential distribution hazard rate \(\lambda_C\). extra record generated minimum latent survival time, random censoring time, administrative censoring time greater number regular treatment cycles times days per cycle. Finally apply lag function \(Z_{,j}\) \(A_{,j}\) represent PD status alternative therapy status start cycle \(j\) (thus remain appplicable entire cycle \(j\)) subject \(\). estimate true treatment effect Cox marginal structural model, one can set \(\alpha_0 = -\infty\), effectively forces \(A_{,j} = 0\) (disabling treatment switching). coefficient randomized treatment can estimated using Cox proportional hazards model.

title: Assess Proportional Hazards Assumption Based on Scaled Schoenfeld Residuals — zph_phregr
text: list following components: table matrix one row parameter final row global test. columns contain score test adding time-dependent term, degrees freedom, two-sided p-value. x transformed time values. time original (untransformed) event times, tied event times repeated. strata stratum index event. y matrix scaled Schoenfeld residuals, one column parameter one row event. Column names correspond parameter names. var approximate covariance matrix scaled Schoenfeld residuals, used construct approximate standard error band plots. transform transformation applied time values.
